title: Corytoplectus deltoideus
intro: Corytoplectus deltoideus, also known as C. deltoideus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of C. deltoideus include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is often used in traditional herbal medicine and can be found in various preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as in some commercial herbal supplements.
taxonomy: Corytoplectus deltoideus is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions of Colombia and Ecuador. As a member of the Gesneriaceae family, Corytoplectus deltoideus is closely related to other epiphytic and terrestrial plants in the same family. The Gesneriaceae family is a large and diverse group, consisting of around 1,400 species. Corytoplectus deltoideus is often classified as a member of the genus Corytoplectus, which is a relatively small genus with a limited number of species.
